title: aconitum japonicum subsp. maritimum
intro: Aconitum japonicum subsp. maritimum, also known as the Japanese monk's hood or sea monk's hood, is a subspecies of Aconitum japonicum, a perennial flowering plant native to Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and analgesic effects. The health benefits associated with Aconitum japonicum subsp. maritimum include pain relief, reduced fever, and improved immune function. Aconitum japonicum subsp. maritimum is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or ointment for topical application, and its herbal preparations are typically used in small doses due to the plant's potential toxicity.
